title: saracha ovata
intro: Saracha ovata, also known as Mexican tea or wild oregano, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties due to its high concentration of carvacrol and thymol, which are compounds commonly found in oregano and other members of the Lamiaceae family. The medicinal properties of Saracha ovata have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, colds, and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Saracha ovata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function.
